meta.rule: All research_hub experiments use CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 (DEVICE_ID default 2).
meta.why: Explicit user request on 2026-04-15. GPU 0 is usually occupied by TOFU unlearn, GPU 1 by a previous wave; GPUs 2/3 were observed idle.
meta.when: Default GPU for every new driver / run.sh / python launcher is 2. Does not apply to the LLDM attack project (sw/) — research_hub scope only.

meta.rule: Loop phases use a flat 7-label set only (A=paper-collection, B=topic-exploration, C=experiment-planning, D=alignment, E=implementation, F=analysis, done). Bundle semantics (planning/alignment/execution) are expressed as tag columns in docs. loop_state.py records phase_schema=2; legacy phases are mapped at read-time via LEGACY_PHASE_MAP. History entries preserve the original (append-only).
meta.why: Stacking hierarchical sub-labels (A-1/A-2/A-3, C-1/C-2) on top of the old 3-stage (A/B/C) caused repeated A-2/A-3 and C-1/C-2 swaps, and there was a label collision between legacy 'B' (alignment) and the new 'B' (topic-exploration). Flat labels + schema version stamp eliminate the ambiguity.
meta.when: New phase mentions must use flat labels. loop_state reads/writes go through the SSOT .claude/scripts/loop_state.py only. Do not edit legacy history entries or archived lessons-* labels (append-only). Apply this rule when introducing new phase labels in harness docs, agents, skills, hooks, and commands.

nodes.name: Loop purpose redefinition — answer user question + evidence + experimental verification

meta.rule: The loop's purpose is to produce an evidence-grounded Direct Answer + Evidence Chain for the user's question and verify each Evidence empirically. Divergent ideation (generating new research topics) is forbidden.
meta.why: Divergent ideation easily drifted away from the user's question, and the experiment plan turned into testing new hypotheses rather than verifying evidence, blurring F-1 accountability. Moving to an Answer-Evidence 1:1:1 trace (Answer<->Evidence<->Experiment) shuts down post-hoc interpretation and claim slippage.
meta.when: Applied at every stage: B-1 (answer-formulator), B-2 (critic 4 axes), C-1 (experiment-planner 1:1 verification design), E-1/E-2 (3-way IMPL_MAP + decide_verdict), F-1 (CONFIRMED/REFUTED/INCONCLUSIVE/IMPL_BUG verdicts). If tempted to invent a new hypothesis, stop and ask: 'is this an experiment that verifies evidence for the user question?'

meta.rule: The orchestrator treats each sub-phase dispatch as a synchronous blocking call and does not return to the caller until loop_state.phase advances. Bash(run_in_background=true), Monitor stream wait, and TaskCreate-and-return patterns are entirely forbidden. The only exception is /codex:review --background + /codex:status polling. Tasks over 30 minutes escalate to the user via abort condition 5 (resource limit).
meta.why: On the first /research-start run on 2026-04-15, the orchestrator declared it would run paper-hunter in background, hallucinated Monitor stream as a wait mechanism, and returned immediately. Actual processes: 0; loop_state stuck at A-1; no new raw.md files. Monitor sends events to the parent, and when a subagent exits any spawned background task is SIGKILL'd. Neither orchestrator.md nor orchestrate/SKILL.md contained block/wait/until tokens, so no contract existed.
meta.when: Every sub-phase dispatch. Especially where runtime is uncertain (A-1 paper-hunter, A-3 paper-summarizer, E-1 code-implementer, E-3/F-2 codex-reviewer). Recall this rule whenever a 'run in background and return' temptation shows up. research-start auto-recovers via up to 2 re-dispatches in autonomous mode if loop_state.phase equals the entry phase after return; beyond that, escalate.

meta.rule: The SSOT for TRIGGER_WHITELIST is the single TRIGGER_WHITELIST constant in loop_state.py; the only user-facing copy is CLAUDE.md §4.3. Do not copy the phrase list into prompt files under .claude/agents or .claude/skills; those files only mention calling loop_state.py trigger-check. Test files live at .claude/tests/ top-level to visually separate them from production .claude/scripts/.
meta.why: During 2026-04-15 codex M2, when ambiguous single-word triggers were removed, the whitelist existed in 3+ places, risking stale copies. Drift across 4 copies breaks Phase B->C trigger-check consistency. Narrowing SSOT to loop_state.py alone and allowing one user-contract copy (CLAUDE.md) limits the drift surface to 2 places; a sync comment forces simultaneous updates. Test artifacts mixed with production scripts create the illusion that 'everything here is production'.
meta.when: When adding/removing trigger phrases, update only the TRIGGER_WHITELIST list in loop_state.py plus §4.3 in CLAUDE.md. Do not copy the list inside .claude/agents or .claude/skills. Trigger judgement always goes through loop_state.py trigger-check. For future harness dedup, run 3-step safety: (a) diff copy contents, (b) trace runtime consumers, (c) confirm Claude-read paths before collapsing.
